test/app/custominvoicing.purpose: Integration tests for the custom invoicing app — validates the async sync protocol (draft→issuing→payment) and invoice event JSON marshaling, exercising the full billing state machine against a real Postgres DB via billingtest.BaseSuite.

patterns.name: Embed billingtest.BaseSuite
patterns.description: All test suites embed billingtest.BaseSuite (not app/common wiring) to get BillingService, CustomerService, AppService, MockStreamingConnector, etc. wired from raw package constructors.

patterns.name: setupDefaultBillingProfile helper
patterns.description: Each test scenario calls s.setupDefaultBillingProfile(ctx, namespace, config) to install the custom invoicing app, configure it, and provision the billing profile before any invoice operations.

patterns.name: Drive state machine via service layer, not adapter
patterns.description: Invoice lifecycle is driven through s.BillingService and s.CustomInvoicingService (SyncDraftInvoice, SyncIssuingInvoice, HandlePaymentTrigger) — never through adapter or Ent directly.

patterns.name: Unique namespace per test function
patterns.description: Every test function uses a unique namespace string literal (e.g. 'ns-custom-invoicing-flow') to avoid cross-test DB contamination without needing teardown.

patterns.name: MockStreamingConnector for usage data
patterns.description: Usage data is injected via s.MockStreamingConnector.AddSimpleEvent before invoicing; always defer s.MockStreamingConnector.Reset() to clean up.

patterns.name: Suite.Run for sub-scenarios
patterns.description: Related invoice lifecycle steps are grouped with s.Run('description', func(){...}) to produce hierarchical test output and share invoice state between steps.
patterns.example: s.Run("invoice can be created", func() { invoices, err := s.BillingService.InvoicePendingLines(...) })

key_file_guides.role: Main integration test: CustomInvoicingTestSuite covers full invoice flow (hooks enabled) and payment-status-only flow (hooks disabled); also provides setupDefaultBillingProfile helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DraftPeriod is set to P0D in setupDefaultBillingProfile so lines immediately become invoiceable — change carefully or invoicing assertions will break.

key_file_guides.role: Unit-level JSON round-trip test for billing.StandardInvoiceCreatedEvent — validates that app bases and appcustominvoicing.Meta survive marshal/unmarshal.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses context.Background() (not t.Context()) — acceptable here because no cancellation semantics are tested; new tests should prefer t.Context().

anti_patterns: Calling adapter or Ent methods directly instead of going through BillingService / CustomInvoicingService
Importing app/common wiring into test setup — use raw package constructors like billingtest.BaseSuite
Sharing namespace strings across test functions — always use a unique namespace per top-level test
Forgetting defer s.MockStreamingConnector.Reset() — stale events bleed into subsequent tests
Setting DraftPeriod to a non-zero duration without adjusting AsOf — lines won't be invoiceable

decisions.decision: Tests embed billingtest.BaseSuite instead of constructing services manually
decisions.rationale: Keeps test setup DRY and independent from app/common to avoid import cycles; BaseSuite provides all shared services from underlying constructors.

decisions.decision: Hook configuration (EnableDraftSyncHook/EnableIssuingSyncHook) is exercised with both true and false to validate the two distinct invoice state paths
decisions.rationale: Custom invoicing has two modes: full async sync (draft→issuing sync) and payment-status-only (skips sync hooks); both paths need explicit coverage.

code_examples.scenario: Full invoice lifecycle with sync hooks enabled

test/app/stripe.purpose: Integration and unit tests for the Stripe app: covers app install/get/update/uninstall lifecycle, customer data CRUD, invoice sync to Stripe, and session creation — all using mock Stripe clients to avoid real API calls.

patterns.name: TestEnv interface for app_test.go
patterns.description: app_test.go and appstripe.go tests use a TestEnv interface (App, AppStripe, Billing, Customer, Fixture, Secret, StripeClient, StripeAppClient, Close) provisioned by NewTestEnv — services are wired manually without app/common.

patterns.name: StripeClientMock / StripeAppClientMock with Restore()
patterns.description: Mock expectations are set per-test with .On(...).Return(...); call defer s.Env.StripeAppClient().Restore() or defer stripeClient.Restore() after each setup to clear expectations without recreating the mock.

patterns.name: Embed billingtest.BaseSuite for invoice tests
patterns.description: StripeInvoiceTestSuite embeds billingtest.BaseSuite and builds extra services (SecretService, AppStripeService) in SetupSuite using raw constructors, not app/common wiring.
patterns.example: type StripeInvoiceTestSuite struct { billingtest.BaseSuite; AppStripeService appstripe.Service }

patterns.name: Fixture for shared setup sequences
patterns.description: fixture.go provides Fixture.setupApp, setupCustomer, setupAppWithCustomer to avoid duplicating Stripe mock setup and app installation across tests.
patterns.example: testApp, customer, customerData, err := s.Env.Fixture().setupAppWithCustomer(ctx, s.namespace)

patterns.name: MockSecretService with passthrough mode
patterns.description: MockSecretService wraps a real secretservice; EnableMock()/DisableMock() toggle mocked vs real behavior — tests that need real secret persistence call DisableMock (the default).

patterns.name: Unique namespace per test via setupNamespace(t)
patterns.description: Each test in AppHandlerTestSuite calls s.setupNamespace(t) which sets s.namespace = ulid.Make().String() to guarantee namespace isolation.

key_file_guides.role: Constructs the complete test dependency graph for app_test.go: Postgres via testutils.InitPostgresDB, customer/app/secret/stripe services wired manually, closerFunc for cleanup.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses context.Background() in NewTestEnv — acceptable for env setup. New services added to the stripe test env must be added to both the testEnv struct and the TestEnv interface.

key_file_guides.role: Implements stripeclient.StripeClient and stripeclient.StripeAppClient with testify/mock; includes StableSortInvoiceItemParams/StableSortStripeInvoiceItemWithID to normalize line item order for deterministic mock matching.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Restore() clears c.ExpectedCalls[:0] — forgetting to call it leaks expectations across tests causing spurious failures.

key_file_guides.role: Deep invoice sync tests: builds Stripe mock call sequences for CreateInvoice/AddInvoiceLines/FinalizeInvoice; verifies Stripe API call shapes match billing line structures.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Stripe mock calls must match exact input structs (including sorted line items) — use StableSortInvoiceItemParams in the mock to make AddInvoiceLines matching deterministic.

key_file_guides.role: Shared setup helpers that install a Stripe app, create a customer, and attach customer data. Uses Restore() via defer after each setup call.
key_file_guides.watch_for: getStripeAccountId generates a random account ID — tests that call setupApp twice in the same test will have different account IDs unless the mock is configured to handle both.

key_file_guides.role: Wraps real secretservice with optional mock interception; default is passthrough (mockEnabled=false).
key_file_guides.watch_for: The var _ secret.SecretService compile check enforces interface completeness — add new methods to both MockSecretService and the real service simultaneously.

anti_patterns: Calling Stripe client methods directly in tests instead of going through app.Service or appstripe.Service
Forgetting defer stripeClient.Restore() / stripeAppClient.Restore() — leftover mock expectations cause subsequent tests to fail
Importing app/common in test env construction — always wire from raw package constructors (appadapter.New, appservice.New, etc.)
Reusing the same namespace across multiple test functions in AppHandlerTestSuite — always call setupNamespace(t) at the start of each test method
Adding invoice line assertions without stable-sorting lines — use StableSortInvoiceItemParams before setting mock expectations on AddInvoiceLines

decisions.decision: Two parallel test architectures: TestEnv interface (app_test.go) vs billingtest.BaseSuite embedding (invoice_test.go)
decisions.rationale: App lifecycle tests (install/update/uninstall) need a narrow env without full billing stack; invoice tests need the full billing stack from BaseSuite — different scopes justified different setups.

decisions.decision: MockSecretService wraps real secretservice with Enable/Disable toggle rather than pure mock
decisions.rationale: Most tests need real secret persistence (storing Stripe API keys); only error-path tests need mock control, so passthrough-by-default avoids brittle expectation setup in the happy path.

decisions.decision: Stripe client mock sorts invoice line items before matching
decisions.rationale: Billing line generation order is non-deterministic; stable sorting in the mock (StableSortInvoiceItemParams) ensures test assertions are order-independent without requiring production code to sort.

code_examples.scenario: Install a Stripe app and verify it can be retrieved
